snippet: NASA announced a new climate monitoring mission that will use advanced satellite technology to study global temperature
full_text: NASA announced a new climate monitoring mission that will use advanced satellite technology to study global temperature changes, greenhouse gas emissions, and environmental impacts. Scientists say the mission will provide valuable data to improve climate models and support research efforts worldwide.

snippet:
NASA's James Webb Space Telescope has captured the deepest and sharpest 
infrared image of the distant universe to date
full_text:
NASA's James Webb Space Telescope has captured the deepest and sharpest 
infrared image of the distant universe to date. The image shows thousands 
of galaxies, including the faintest objects ever observed. Scientists at 
NASA, ESA, and the Canadian Space Agency released the image during a 
White House briefing. The telescope, launched in December 2021, is designed 
to look back over 13.5 billion years to see the first stars and galaxies 
that formed after the Big Bang.

full_text:
President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Friday directing 
federal agencies to reduce regulations. The order requires that for 
every new regulation introduced, two existing regulations must be 
eliminated. White House officials said the move is intended to reduce 
the regulatory burden on businesses and stimulate economic growth. 
The administration expects the policy to save billions of dollars 
annually for American companies.
